revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Amamikoso-jinja]][[ja:йҳҝйә»зҫҺиЁұжӣҫзҘһзӨҫ]] {{Expand Japanese|йҳҝйә»зҫҺиЁұжӣҫзҘһзӨҫ}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Amamikoso Shrine | native_name = йҳҝйә»зҫҺиЁұжӣҫзҘһзӨҫ | image = Amamikoso-jinja02.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Amamikoso Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.595436|135.529697}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|ШіЩҲШіШ§ЩҶЩҲ|azb|ШіЩҲШіШ§ЩҶЩҲ|bcl|Susanoo|be|РЎСғСҒР°РҪР°Р°|bg|РЎСғСҒР°РҪРҫРҫ|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|ОЈОҝП…ПғО¬ОҪoОҝ|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|ШіЩҲШіШ§ЩҶЩҲ (Ш®ШҜШ§)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|ХҚХёЦӮХҪХЎХ¶Хё|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ|ka|бғЎбғЈбғЎбғҗбғңбғқбғқ-бғңбғқ-бғӣбғҳбғҷбғқбғўбғқ|ko|мҠӨмӮ¬л…ёмҳӨ|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|кҜҒкҜЁкҜҒкҜ…кҜЁ-кҜ…кҜЈ-кҜғкҜӨкҜҖкҜЈкҜҮкҜЈ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|РЎСғСҒР°РҪРҫРҫ|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|РЎСғСҒР°РҪРҫРҫ|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|а®ҡаҜҒа®ҡа®ҫа®©а®өаҜӢ|th|аёӢаё№аёӢаёІа№Ӯаёҷа№Ӯаёӯаё°|tr|Susanoo|uk|РЎСғСҒР°РҪРҫРҫ|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|зҙ жҲ”е—ҡе°Ҡ|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | website = {{Official website|http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/higashisumiyoshi/page/0000032765.html}} }} {{nihongo|'''Amamikoso Shrine'''|йҳҝйә»зҫҺиЁұжӣҫзҘһзӨҫ}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|Ш¬ЩҠЩҶШ¬Ш§|arz|Ш¶ШұЩҠШӯ ШҙЩҶШӘЩҲ|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Е intoistickГЎ svatynДӣ|da|Shinto-tempel|de|ShintЕҚ-Schrein|el|ОЈО№ОҪП„ОҝПҠПғП„О№ОәПҢ ОҷОөПҒПҢ|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Еңintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|Щ…Ш№ШЁШҜ ШҙЫҢЩҶШӘЩҲЫҢЫҢ|fi|Е intolainen pyhГӨkkГ¶|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|аӨ¶аӨҝаӨӮаӨӨаҘӢ аӨ®аӨӮаӨҰаӨҝаӨ°|hr|Е intoistiДҚki hram|hu|SintГі szentГ©ly|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|ka|бғ«бғҳбғңбғ«бғҳбғҗ|ko|мӢ мӮ¬ (мӢ нҶ )|lb|ShintЕҚ-SchrГӨin|mr|аӨ¶аӨҝаӨӮаӨӨаҘӢ аӨ®аӨӮаӨҰаӨҝаӨ°|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|а¬ңа¬ҝа¬һаӯҚа¬ңа¬ҫ|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoГӯsta|ru|Р”Р·РёРҪРҙР·СҸ|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto ГҮhapaan|hak|NgiМҚt-pГәn|ha|Japan|haw|IДҒpana|he|ЧҷЧӨЧҹ|henews|ЧӘЧ’ЧҷЧӘ:ЧҷЧӨЧҹ|hequote|ЧҷЧӨЧҹ|hevoyage|ЧҷЧӨЧҹ|hif|Japan|hi|аӨңаӨҫаӨӘаӨҫаӨЁ|hiquote|аӨңаӨҫаӨӘаӨҫаӨЁ|hivoyage|аӨңаӨҫаӨӘаӨҫаӨЁ|hr|Japan|hsb|Japanska|ht|Japon|hu|JapГЎn|hunews|KategГіria:JapГЎn|hy|ХғХЎХәХёХ¶Х«ХЎ|hyw|ХғХЎЦғХёХ¶|ia|Japon|iba|Jipun|id|Jepang|idquote|Jepang|idvoyage|Jepang|ie|Japan|igl|Japan|ig|Japan|ik|Nippan|ilo|Hapon|inh|РҜРҝРҫРҪРөС…СҢРө|io|Japonia|is|Japan|it|Giappone|itnews|Categoria:Giappone|itquote|Giappone|itvoyage|Giappone|iu|б”ӯбҗ№б“җ|jam|Japan|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬|janews|гӮ«гғҶгӮҙгғӘ:ж—Ҙжң¬|jaquote|ж—Ҙжң¬|javoyage|ж—Ҙжң¬|jbo|pongu'e|jv|Jepang|kaa|Yaponiya|kab|Yapan|ka|бғҳбғҗбғһбғқбғңбғҳбғҗ|kbd|РҜРҝРҫРҪ|kbp|SapЙ”ЕӢ|kcg|JaМұpan|kge|Jopang|kg|Japan|ki|Japan|kk|Р–Р°РҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|km|бһҮбһ”бҹүбһ»бһ“|knc|Japan|kn|аІңаІӘаІҫаІЁаіҚ|koi|РқРёРҝРҝРҫРҪ|ko|мқјліё|konews|л¶„лҘҳ:мқјліё|koquote|мқјліё|krc|Р”Р¶Р°РҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|ksh|Japan|ks|Ш¬Ш§ЩҫШ§ЩҶ|ku|Japon|kv|РҜРҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|kw|Nihon|ky|Р–Р°РҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|lad|Japon|la|Iaponia|lbe|РҜРҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|lb|Japan|lez|РҜРҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|lfn|Nion|lg|Japan|lij|Giappon|li|Japan|lld|Iapan|lmo|Giapon|ln|ZapЙ”МҒ|lo|аәӣаә°а»Җаә—аә”аәҚаәөа»Ҳаәӣаәёа»Ҳаәҷ|ltg|Japoneja|lt|Japonija|lv|JapДҒna|mad|Jeppang|mai|аӨңаӨҫаӨӘаӨҫаӨЁ|map_bms|Jepang|mdf|РҜРҝРҫРҪРјР°СҒСӮРҫСҖ|mg|JapГ na|mhr|РҜРҝРҫРҪРёР№|min|Japang|mi|Nipono|mk|РҲР°РҝРҫРҪРёСҳР°|ml|аҙңаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙҫаө»|mni|кҜ–кҜ„кҜҘкҜҹ|mn|РҜРҝРҫРҪ|mrj|РҜРҝРҫРҪРё|mr|аӨңаӨӘаӨҫаӨЁ|ms|Jepun|msquote|Jepun|mt|Д appun|mwl|Japon|myv|РҜРҝРҫРҪРёСҸ бҘҷбҘЈбҘўбҘұ|tet|Japaun|te|а°ңа°Әа°ҫа°ЁаұҚ|tg|Т¶РҫРҝРҫРҪ|th|аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё—аёЁаёҚаёөа№Ҳаёӣаёёа№Ҳаёҷ|thnews|аё«аёЎаё§аё”аё«аёЎаё№а№Ҳ:аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё—аёЁаёҚаёөа№Ҳаёӣаёёа№Ҳаёҷ|tig|бҢғбҚ“бҠ•|ti|бҢғбҚ“бҠ•|tk|ГқaponiГҪa|tl|Hapon|tly|JaponijЙҷ|tn|Japan|tok|ma Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|РҜРҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|РҜРҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|ty|TДҒpЕҚnД“|udm|РҜРҝРҫРҪРёСҸ|ug|ЩҠШ§ЩҫЩҲЩҶЩүЩҠЫ•|uk|РҜРҝРҫРҪС–СҸ|uknews|РҡР°СӮРөРіРҫСҖС–СҸ:РҜРҝРҫРҪС–СҸ|ukquote|РҜРҝРҫРҪС–СҸ|ukvoyage|РҜРҝРҫРҪС–СҸ|ur|Ш¬Ш§ЩҫШ§ЩҶ|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhбәӯt BбәЈn|vibooks|CГЎc quб»‘c gia/Nhбәӯt BбәЈn|vivoyage|Nhбәӯt BбәЈn|vls|Japan|vo|YapГӨn|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|SapoЕӢ|wuu|ж—Ҙжң¬|xal|РқРёxСғСғРҙРёРҪ РқСғСӮРі|xh|IJapani|xmf|бғҳбғҗбғһбғқбғңбғҳбғҗ|yi|ЧҷЧҗЧӨЧҗЧҹ|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|вөҸвөүвҙұвө“вөҸ|zh_classical|ж—Ҙжң¬|zh_min_nan|JiМҚt-pГәn|zh_yue|ж—Ҙжң¬|zh|ж—Ҙжң¬|zhnews|Category:ж—Ҙжң¬|zhquote|ж—Ҙжң¬|zhvoyage|ж—Ҙжң¬|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * гҒӮгҒҫгҒҝгҒ“гҒқгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1120005001798 == Commons category (P373) == * Amamikoso-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': 'еӨ§йҳӘеәңеӨ§йҳӘеёӮжқұдҪҸеҗүеҢәзҹўз”°7-6', 'language': 'ja'}http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/toshikeikaku/page/0000250227.html * {'text': 'еӨ§йҳӘеёӮжқұдҪҸеҗүеҢәзҹўз”°', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Amamikoso-jinja}} [[JA:йҳҝйә»зҫҺиЁұжӣҫзҘһзӨҫ]] {{wikidata link|Q11658015}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name=Amamikoso Shrine | Image=[[File:Amamikoso-jinja02.JPG|260px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Higashisumiyoshi Ward|Q861264|qid=Q861264|1=Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Yata (Osaka City)|Q80932676|qid=Q80932676|lt=Yata|1=Yata}} 7-chЕҚme | Coordinates={{гӮҰгӮЈгӮӯеә§жЁҷ2ж®өеәҰеҲҶз§’|34|35|44|N|135|31|45|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=GЕҚsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=GЕҚ-sha}} | Founded=Early 9th century ({{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=DaidЕҚ|1=DaidЕҚ}} era) | Main hall=Amami-zukuri | Annual festivals=Ebisu Festival (1/7вҖ“8)
Summer Festival (7/16вҖ“17)
Autumn Festival (10/16вҖ“17) | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amamikoso
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amamikoso Shrine''' (гҒӮгҒҫгҒҝгҒ“гҒқгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yata (Osaka City)|Q80932676|qid=Q80932676|lt=Yata|1=Yata}}, {{ill|lt=Higashisumiyoshi Ward|Q861264|qid=Q861264|1=Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It is a shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|qid=Q11368538|1=TajihinokЕҚri district}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|qid=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=GЕҚsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=GЕҚ-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611}} At present, the enshrined deities are the three listed above; however, the вҖңKongЕҚji-bonвҖқ records only one seat, making the original principal deity unclear. Because this area was under the control of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|qid=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, it has been conjecturedвҖ”considering that вҖңKosoвҖқ (гҒ“гҒқ) in the shrineвҖҷs name may denote an honorificвҖ”that the Nakatomi clanвҖҷs Amanohiko-no-Mikoto was the original principal deity. On the other hand, according to the {{ill|lt=Sendai Kujihongi|Q2716751|qid=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}вҖҷs Hikomisone-no-Mikoto was appointed as the provincial ruler of Kawachi and was вҖңburied at Amami Hill in Tanbi.вҖқ From this, another theory suggests that Hikomisone-no-Mikoto was originally enshrined here, and that after the Mononobe clanвҖҷs downfall, the Nakatomi clan occupied the area and replaced the enshrined deity {{Cite web|Japanese book| url = https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/higashisumiyoshi/page/0000032765.html | title = Osaka City Higashisumiyoshi Ward 003 Amamikoso Shrine | date = 2013-07-04 | publisher = {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Higashisumiyoshi Ward|Q861264|qid=Q861264|1=Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}} | accessdate = 2015-09-13}}. === History === Amamikoso Shrine is a shikinaisha, and according to shrine tradition, it was founded during the DaidЕҚ era (around DaidЕҚ 1 = 806), though it is believed to be older. вҖңKosoвҖқ is said to derive from a word in {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|qid=Q28456|1=Silla}}, and it is thought that immigrants from the {{ill|lt=Korean Peninsula|Q483134|qid=Q483134|1=Korean Peninsula}} may have begun the shrine by worshipping their ancestors {{Cite web|Japanese book| url = http://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/10,113,51,258.html | title = History Walk: 18 Amamikoso Shrine and Immigrants | date = 2009-01-09 | publisher = {{ill|lt=Matsubara City|Q774952|qid=Q774952|1=Matsubara}} | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20130704105411/http://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/10,113,51,258.html | archivedate = 2013-07-04 | accessdate = 2015-09-13}}. To the east of the purification pavilion stands a stone monument inscribed вҖңPlace where GyЕҚki Bosatsu resided,вҖқ and there is a tradition that {{ill|lt=GyЕҚki|Q1136664|qid=Q1136664|1=GyЕҚki}} lived here. However, this tradition appears only from around the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} , and since no such record appears in the вҖңGyЕҚki Chronology,вҖқ it is considered difficult to substantiate . The area including this shrine was originally part of Yata Village (later becoming part of Osaka City). During the Edo period, the rerouting of the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|qid=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}} split Yata Village, leaving the shrine separated on the riverвҖҷs south bank. At that time, most of the southern part of Yata Village was incorporated into what later became Matsubara City, but because the shrine was the tutelary shrine of Yata Village, only the area around the shrine and its approach remained part of Yata Village. As a result, although the shrine lies on the south bank of the Yamato River, it belongs to Osaka City, and the section corresponding to the approach (the {{ill|lt=KЕҚya KaidЕҚ|Q11673149|qid=Q11673149|lt=Lower KЕҚya KaidЕҚ|1=KЕҚya KaidЕҚ}}) extends narrowly into Matsubara City while remaining part of Osaka City {{Cite web|Japanese book| url = http://portal.nifty.com/kiji/150312192969_2.htm | title = The Mystery of Osaka CityвҖҷs Extremely Narrow Territory and the Kofun Curve 2 | date = 2015-03-13 | author = Akira ЕҢyama | publisher = Daily Portal Z | accessdate = 2015-09-13}}. Until the early Meiji period, a {{ill|lt=jingЕ«-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=JingЕ«-ji}} with the mountain name вҖңAmamiyamaвҖқ stood within the precincts, preserving a history of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shЕ«gЕҚ|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shЕ«gЕҚ}}; however, it was abolished during the {{ill|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|qid=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} movement. The templeвҖҷs gate nonetheless remains along the approach . === Shrine buildings === The current worship hall was built in {{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|qid=Q7701|1=1863}} (BunkyЕ« 3), and the main hall, in the distinctive Amami-zukuri style, was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1960|Q3754|qid=Q3754|1=1960}} (ShЕҚwa 35) . Within the grounds are a gigantic {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} over 500 years old and a statue of a sacred horse. === Other === The place name Amami in {{ill|lt=Matsubara City|Q774952|qid=Q774952|1=Matsubara}} derives from Amami. Although the shrine lies within Osaka City, it serves as the tutelary shrine of the Amami area of Matsubara City . On the other hand, as mentioned above, there are also descriptions identifying it as the tutelary shrine of Yata Village . Together with {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|qid=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|qid=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, GadЕҚ Hachiman Shrine, {{ill|lt=Miyake Shrine|Q11465762|qid=Q11465762|1=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)}}, and {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|qid=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}}, the six shrines conduct the вҖңKaiun Matsubara Six Shrines PilgrimageвҖқ [https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Kaiun Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara City Tourism Association. The five shrines other than Amamikoso Shrine are located within Matsubara City. === Access === * Get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|qid=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Amami Station|Q6379447|qid=Q6379447|1=Kawachi-Amami Station}}; about a 20-minute walk * From {{ill|lt=Osaka Municipal Subway|Q51211653|qid=Q51211653|1=Osaka Metro (company)}} (Osaka Metro) {{ill|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|Q1192413|qid=Q1192413|lt=Midosuji Line|1=MidЕҚsuji Line}} {{ill|lt=Abiko Station (Osaka)|Q2084534|qid=Q2084534|lt=Abiko Station|1=Abiko Station (Osaka)}}, take the {{ill|lt=Hokko KankЕҚ Bus|Q11403750|qid=Q11403750|1=Hokkoh Kanko Bus}} (bound for Amami Kita 2-chЕҚme) and get off at Amami Shrine-mae === Notes === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Amamikoso-jinja}} * [http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=34/35/30.267&el=135/31/56.667&scl=25000&bid=Mlink Reference map (from Mapion)] {{Shinto nav}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Osaka Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amamikoso Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amamikoso Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amamikoso Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai ShЕҚsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:дё№еҢ—йғЎ|зӨҫгҒӮгҒҫгҒҝгҒ“гҒқгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„]] [[Category:Nakakawachi District]] [[Category:жқұдҪҸеҗүеҢәгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:жқұдҪҸеҗүеҢәгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:History of Matsubara City]] [[Category:зҹўз”° (еӨ§йҳӘеёӮ)]] [[Category:Nakakawachi District]] [[Category:дё№еҢ—йғЎ]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:incorrect template category applied]]
